c74object.name: tap.303.seq~
c74object.description: The tap.303.seq~ object is a step-sequenced bass line speaking exactly the tap.303~ note contract: feed it a phase ramp (0..1 per pattern cycle — a phasor~) and it emits the voice's inlet pair, a pitch signal (MIDI note number, held between notes) and a gate signal whose edge amplitude encodes accent (1.0 plain, 2.0 accented; the accent amount stays on the voice's knob, like the hardware). The gate opens at the step start and closes halfway through the step (Open303's measured duty). A step with the slide flag is approached legato: the gate holds through the boundary while the pitch steps, and the voice's ~60 ms glide does the rest — no retrigger, chained slides chain, and a slide across the pattern wrap works. Per-step data mirrors the hardware pattern memory (pitch, gate/rest, accent, slide; pitch as a MIDI float subsumes the octave up/down flags), without recreating the 1981 entry workflow: patterns are lists, step messages, sixteen slots with boundary-quantized recall, and dictionaries. Rows share the phase-clock idiom with tap.808.seq~ — one phasor runs a whole rhythm section in sample lock, and this row's gate edge also triggers any tap.808.* voice directly. The design of record is plans/tap.seq.md.

inlet.digest: Phase ramp: 0..1 per pattern cycle (connect phasor~)

outlet.digest: Pitch as MIDI note number — to tap.303~'s left inlet
Gate; edge amplitude 1..2 encodes accent — to tap.303~'s right inlet
Current step number (1-based), sent at each step change

method.name: signal
method.description: The phase ramp. The row derives the current step from the phase (swing warps the odd-step starts) and plays the pattern sample-accurately. Stateless against phase: jump, scrub, or reverse the ramp and the line follows.

method.name: step
method.description: Set one step and gate it on: step number (1..64), pitch as a MIDI note number (fractional = cents), then optional accent and slide flags (0/1). A slide flag means the step is approached legato from the note before it.

method.name: rest
method.description: Silence one step (its pitch and flags are kept).

method.name: pitches
method.description: Set per-step pitches as MIDI note numbers (fractional = cents).

method.name: gates
method.description: Set which steps sound: one 0/1 per step (0 = rest).

method.name: accents
method.description: Set per-step accent flags: accented steps gate at 2.0 (the voice reads accent depth = amplitude - 1).

method.name: slides
method.description: Set per-step slide flags. A flagged step is approached legato: the gate holds through the boundary and the voice glides to it without retriggering.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the pattern in a slot (1..16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Recall a slot (1..16); it takes over at the quantize boundary (default: the next pattern top).

method.name: todict
method.description: Write the pattern (length, pitches, gates, accents, slides) into a named dictionary, for saving with the patcher or passing around as data.

method.name: fromdict
method.description: Restore a pattern previously written with todict.

method.name: clear
method.description: Empty the pattern (all rests) and reset the clock state.

attribute.name: length
attribute.description: Steps per pattern cycle. A different length than the other rows on the same phasor is polymeter.

attribute.name: swing
attribute.description: Odd-numbered steps play late by up to half a step. The hardware had no swing — 0 is stock.

attribute.name: transpose
attribute.description: Applied as notes fire — what the hardware's transpose mode did, without the mode.

attribute.name: quantize
attribute.digest: When a recalled pattern takes over: cycle (default), step, or now
attribute.description: cycle swaps at the next pattern top, step at the next step boundary, now immediately.

attribute.name: mute
attribute.digest: Force the gate low (the clock keeps running; pitch holds)
attribute.description: Mutes the gate without stopping the row, so unmuting re-enters in place.
